paper.title: A high-velocity star recently ejected by an intermediate-mass black hole in M15

extraction.summary: The paper reports J0731+3717 as a high-velocity star ejected from globular cluster M15 via the Hills mechanism (V_GSR = 419 km/s, ejection velocity 548 km/s). However, the paper does not explicitly discuss J0731+3717 as possibly unbound from the Milky Way / Galactic potential. Throughout the text the object is consistently labelled a 'high-velocity star' (not a 'hypervelocity star'), and the paper explicitly contrasts 'hypervelocity stars from the Galactic center' with 'high-velocity stars from the IMBHs in the center of globular clusters'. No other objects are presented as Galactic-unbound candidates.

candidate_groups_considered.description: The main object J0731+3717 and its parameters in Table 1. The paper also discusses a sample of 934 high-velocity halo stars with V_GSR >= 400 km/s used for the systematic search, but only J0731+3717 is examined in detail.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: The paper presents J0731+3717 as a high-velocity star ejected from the globular cluster M15, not as a candidate that is possibly unbound from the Milky Way. The paper explicitly distinguishes 'hypervelocity stars from the Galactic center' (unbound) from 'high-velocity stars from the IMBHs in the center of globular clusters' (this object). V_GSR = 419 km/s is reported without any bound/unbound assessment against the Galactic potential. The other 934 high-velocity stars in the search sample are likewise not presented as Galactic-unbound candidates.

source_refs.context: Abstract describes J0731+3717 as a high-velocity star ejected from M15; no Galactic-unbound claim is made.
Results section labels J0731+3717 as a high-velocity star (V_GSR = 419 km/s) ejected from M15; discusses orbital intersection with M15, not Galactic escape.
Paper explicitly contrasts Hills-mechanism hypervelocity stars from the Galactic center with high-velocity stars from globular-cluster IMBHs, using the latter label for J0731+3717.
